texto: The old school of thought would have you believe that you'd be a fool to take on nature without arming yourself with every conceivable measure of safety and comfort under the sun. But that isn't what being in nature is all about. Rather, it's about feeling free, unbounded, shedding the distractions and barriers of our civilization—not bringing them with us.
explicacao: Citação de Ryel Kestenbaum sobre temas ligados a educacao. “The old school of thought would have you believe that you'd be a fool to take on nature without a…” articula educacao, backpacking, barriers em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como barriers e burden.
